source_issue.title: Integrate missing information about layers and traversing

summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue addresses the need to improve the documentation regarding layers and traversing in Plone. Specifically, there's a lack of explanation around the role of `IDefaultBrowserLayer` as a base class for Layers.

## What needs to be done
You are invited to integrate the background information provided in a related discussion into the documentation. This information should clarify why `IDefaultBrowserLayer` is significant and ensure it is documented in the correct place: `docs/backend/traversal-acquisition.md`.

## Helpful background
The original discussion can be found [here](https://github.com/plone/bobtemplates.plone/issues/89#issuecomment-138474557), where the importance of `IDefaultBrowserLayer` is outlined. The insights are backed by real code snippets and tested examples from demo.plone.org. While the discussion provides valuable context, it may contain some ambiguity or outdated elements.

## Good first steps
1. Read through the linked discussion to understand the current explanations provided.
2. Review the existing documentation in `docs/backend/traversal-acquisition.md` to see where updates are needed.
3. Draft improvements to the documentation based on the gathered information.
4. Consider reaching out for feedback on your draft before finalizing your contributions.

Your effort in refining the documentation will help both current and future Plone users!
